_comment: Hand-authored MCP tool description overrides for HaloPSA workflow-critical entities. Covers the ~60 tools an agent will reach for most often (tickets, actions, clients, agents, assets, sites, users, KB, SLA, contracts, time, teams, configs). The full 1456-tool surface will be addressed upstream via printing-press#1754 (generator-side description composer).

descriptions.tickets_list: List HaloPSA tickets (Faults) for the authenticated tenant. Optional filters include agent_id, team_id, client_id, status, priority_id, search (text match across summary + details), advanced_search (JSON DSL), datesearch (created/updated window), and pageinate (page_no + page_size). Returns a paginated array of ticket objects with id, summary, status, agent, client, dates, and SLA fields. Use this as the primary entry point for triage, queue audit, and reporting workflows.
descriptions.tickets_get: Fetch a single HaloPSA ticket by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails (full details/actions/attachments), includeextraobjects (linked CMDB items + custom fields). Returns the full ticket payload including conversation history, asset links, time entries, and approval state. Prefer this over `tickets_list` when you already have an id and need the complete context.
descriptions.tickets_create: Create one or more HaloPSA tickets. Body is an array of ticket objects; minimum required fields are summary, client_id, and user_id. Optional: details, priority_id, status_id, team_id, agent_id, category_1..4, asset_ids, custom_fields. Returns the new tickets including server-assigned ids. Use for inbound channel intake, escalations from automated monitoring, or bulk-import scenarios.
descriptions.tickets_delete: Hard-delete a HaloPSA ticket by id. Required: id. Optional: reason (free text logged on the deletion). Destructive  -  the ticket and its actions, attachments, and time entries are removed. Prefer closing the ticket (status_id transition) for normal workflow; reserve delete for spam, accidental duplicates, or data-cleanup.
descriptions.actions_list: List actions (conversation events, status changes, time entries, attachments) on HaloPSA tickets. Optional: ticket_id (scope to one ticket), agentonly, actoutcome, actoutcomenum, datesearch. Returns an ordered array of action records with who, when, outcome, body, hours, and visibility flags. Use to reconstruct ticket history, audit agent activity, or feed analytics on response times and outcomes.
descriptions.actions_get: Fetch one HaloPSA action by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails. Returns the full action payload including body, outcome, agent, hours logged, and any linked email/attachment. Use after `actions_list` when you need the full body of a specific reply or note.
descriptions.actions_create: Post one or more actions to existing HaloPSA tickets. Body is an array of action objects; each requires ticket_id and an outcome. Common optional fields: note (markdown/HTML body), hours, billable, hiddenfromuser, send_email (notify the end user), attachments. Returns the new actions with server ids. Use to add replies, internal notes, status transitions, or bulk time entries.
descriptions.actions_delete: Delete a HaloPSA action by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  the action is removed from the ticket history. Use sparingly; prefer marking an action hidden if the issue is visibility rather than authenticity.
descriptions.client_list: List HaloPSA clients (organizations / accounts in the Area table). Optional: search (name match), activeinactive (default active), accountmanageronly (scope to the auth user's accounts), domain, includedetails. Returns a paginated array of clients with id, name, domain, account_manager, billing site, and contract refs. Use as the entry point for client lookup, billing prep, and per-client reporting.
descriptions.client_get: Fetch one HaloPSA client by id. Required: id. Optional: domain, includedetails (full record + custom fields), getavailable_categories. Returns the complete client payload including contacts, sites, contracts, custom fields, and billing config. Use after `client_list` to drill into a specific account.
descriptions.client_create: Create a HaloPSA client (Area record). Body is an array of client objects; minimum required field is name. Optional: main_site, contract_template, account_manager_id, billing_template, custom_fields. Returns the new clients with server-assigned ids. Use for onboarding flows, M&A imports, or partner-portal provisioning.
descriptions.client_delete: Delete a HaloPSA client by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  the client and its sites, users, assets, contracts, and tickets become orphaned or are removed depending on tenant config. Prefer marking the client inactive (`inactive=true` on update) for normal off-boarding.
descriptions.agent_list: List HaloPSA agents (technicians/users with helpdesk login, Uname table). Optional: activeinactive, departmentid, teamid, includedetails. Returns an array of agents with id, name, email, team(s), department, role, and skills. Use for assignment lookups, capacity planning, and team rosters.
descriptions.agent_get: Fetch one HaloPSA agent by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails. Returns the full agent profile including teams, departments, role, certifications, custom fields, and notification preferences. Use to resolve assignee names, check availability, or audit permissions.
descriptions.agent_create: Create a HaloPSA agent (technician). Body is an array of agent objects; minimum required fields are name and emailaddress. Optional: teamids, departmentid, role_id, supervisorid, license_type, sso_user_id. Returns the new agents with ids. Most tenants gate agent creation behind admin permissions.
descriptions.agent_delete: Delete a HaloPSA agent by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  orphans the agent's tickets, time entries, and audit history (they remain attributed by id). Prefer marking the agent inactive for normal off-boarding; reserve delete for ghost accounts or data-cleanup.
descriptions.asset_list: List HaloPSA assets (CMDB items in the Device table  -  laptops, servers, licenses, configurations). Optional: client_id, site_id, assetgroup_id, assettype_id, search (matches name/inventory_number/tag), assetstatus, includedetails. Returns paginated array with id, name, tag, type, client, site, status, and key custom fields. Use for asset lookups during ticket triage, contract scoping, and inventory reporting.
descriptions.asset_get: Fetch one HaloPSA asset by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails, includesoftware, includerelations. Returns the full asset record including custom fields, relationships (parent/child), assigned tickets, contract refs, and software install list. Use to load complete CMDB context for a ticket or for asset audits.
descriptions.asset_create: Create a HaloPSA asset. Body is an array of asset objects; minimum required fields are assettype_id and either client_id or site_id. Optional: name, inventory_number, status, owner_id, custom_fields, parent_assetid. Returns the new assets with ids. Use for monitoring-integration intake, manual onboarding, or bulk-CSV imports.
descriptions.asset_delete: Delete a HaloPSA asset by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  removes the asset and its history; linked tickets are not deleted but lose the asset reference. Prefer marking the asset decommissioned (status transition) for normal lifecycle; reserve delete for accidental duplicates.
descriptions.site_list: List HaloPSA sites (physical/logical locations under a client). Optional: client_id, search, includedetails. Returns array of sites with id, name, client_id, address, phone, and main_contact. Use for assignment scoping (per-site SLAs), shipping/dispatch workflows, and per-site asset rollups.
descriptions.site_get: Fetch one HaloPSA site by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails, includeusers. Returns the full site record including address, contacts, main user, billing site flag, and custom fields. Use to resolve site names or pull site-level configuration.
descriptions.site_create: Create a HaloPSA site. Body is an array of site objects; minimum required fields are name and client_id. Optional: address fields, main_contact_id, sla_id, custom_fields. Returns the new sites with ids. Use for client expansion (new office), or in onboarding scripts that hydrate the client → site → user tree.
descriptions.site_delete: Delete a HaloPSA site by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  removes the site and reassigns/orphans its users and assets per tenant config. Prefer marking the site inactive for off-boarding; reserve delete for accidental creates.
descriptions.users_list: List HaloPSA end-users (customer contacts in the Users table, distinct from `agent_list`). Optional: client_id, site_id, search (matches name + email), activeinactive, vipusersonly, includedetails. Returns paginated array of users with id, name, email, phone, client, site, and VIP/manager flags. Use for end-user lookups during ticket creation, password-reset workflows, and contact directories.
descriptions.users_get: Fetch one HaloPSA end-user by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails, includehistory. Returns the full user profile including email, phone, client/site, custom fields, recent tickets, and self-service portal status. Use to load full caller context when answering a ticket.
descriptions.users_create: Create a HaloPSA end-user contact. Body is an array of user objects; minimum required fields are name and client_id. Optional: emailaddress, phonenumber, site_id, isimportantcontact, custom_fields. Returns the new users with ids. Use for client-onboarding scripts, SCIM imports, or auto-creation from inbound email when sender_unknown.
descriptions.users_delete: Delete a HaloPSA end-user by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  orphans the user's tickets; portal access is revoked. Prefer marking the user inactive for off-boarding; reserve delete for spam/test accounts.
descriptions.kbarticle_list: List HaloPSA Knowledge Base articles. Optional: search (full-text across title+body+resolution), category_id, type (general/internal/customer), activeinactive, includedetails. Returns paginated array with id, name (title), category, owner, status, view_count, and rating. Use for KB lookup during ticket triage, content-audit reports, and KB-suggest workflows. For semantic ranking against an open ticket, prefer the local CLI `halopsa-cli kbarticle suggest` command.
descriptions.kbarticle_get: Fetch one KB article by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails. Returns the full article record including title, body (HTML/markdown), resolution, related articles, attachments, and vote totals. Use after `kbarticle_list` when you need the full article body.
descriptions.kbarticle_create: Create a HaloPSA Knowledge Base article. Body is an array of article objects; minimum required fields are name and category_id. Optional: description, resolution, type, status, owner_id, related_articles, customfields. Returns the new articles with ids. Use for KB authoring pipelines, ticket-to-KB conversions, and imports from external KB systems.
descriptions.kbarticle_create-vote: Record an up- or down-vote on a KB article. Body: { articleid, vote: 1 for up / -1 for down, userid (optional) }. Returns the updated vote totals. Use to wire up agent or customer feedback signals on KB content.
descriptions.kbarticle_delete: Delete a KB article by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  removes the article and its votes/attachments. Prefer setting status to archived/inactive for retired articles; reserve delete for duplicates and spam.
descriptions.sla_list: List HaloPSA SLA policies (SlaHead). Optional: access_control_level, isconfig, showpriorities. Returns array of SLAs with id, name, default flag, priority targets (response + resolution times by priority), and assigned clients. Use as the lookup for SLA-policy audits or to resolve sla_id values from `tickets_*` payloads.
descriptions.sla_get: Fetch one SLA policy by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails. Returns the full SLA including all priority-specific response/resolution targets, escalation rules, business-hours config, and pause-condition definitions. Use for SLA-audit workflows or when configuring a new client's policy.
descriptions.sla_create: Create a HaloPSA SLA policy. Body is an array of SLA objects; minimum required field is name. Optional: response_time + resolve_time arrays keyed by priority, businesshours_id, escalation_rules, default. Returns the new SLAs with ids. Most tenants create SLAs through the UI; this is for migrations and provisioning scripts.
descriptions.sla_delete: Delete an SLA policy by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  assigned clients fall back to the tenant default SLA. Audit which clients reference the SLA before deleting.
descriptions.client-contract_list: List HaloPSA client contracts (ContractHeader). Optional: client_id, device_id (per-asset coverage), contract_status, count. Returns paginated array with id, ref, client_id, status, contract_type, period (start/end), hours bank, billing template, and renewal flags. Use as the lookup for billing-prep, contract burn-down (see `halopsa-cli contracts burn`), and renewal pipelines.
descriptions.client-contract_get: Fetch one client contract by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails. Returns the full contract including all line items, prepay balance, charge rate, asset/site scope, renewal terms, and signed-off state. Use to load full contract context for invoicing or for the keystone `clients card` view.
descriptions.client-contract_create: Create a HaloPSA client contract. Body is an array of contract objects; minimum required fields are client_id and contracttype_name. Optional: billing_period, chargehoursperperiod (prepay hours), start_date/end_date, contract_lines, billing_template_id. Returns the new contracts with ids. Use for sales-handoff automation, contract renewals, and migrations.
descriptions.client-contract_delete: Delete a client contract by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  billing rules tied to this contract are removed; affected tickets fall back to next-applicable contract. Prefer ending the contract via end_date for normal lifecycle.
descriptions.timesheet_list: List HaloPSA timesheets and time-entry summaries. Optional: agent_id, datestart/dateend, includesignedoff. Returns array of timesheet records with agent, period, total hours (billable + non-billable), submission state, and per-day breakdown. Use for timesheet audits and submission reports.
descriptions.timesheet_get: Fetch one timesheet by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails (per-action breakdown). Returns the full timesheet including daily totals, per-ticket breakdown, signoff state, and any rejection reasons. Use to load full context before approving or rejecting.
descriptions.timesheet_create: Create or update a timesheet entry. Body is an array of timesheet objects. Use for bulk-import from external time-tracking, scheduled timesheet creation, or programmatic submission. To log time on a specific ticket, prefer `actions_create` with hours/billable fields instead  -  that's the more common per-ticket time-entry pattern.
descriptions.timesheet_list-mine: List the authenticated agent's own timesheets. No client/agent filter required  -  returns this agent's records. Use for the personal timesheet view; for cross-agent audits use `timesheet_list`.
descriptions.team_list: List HaloPSA teams (SectionDetail  -  helpdesk groups). Optional: department_id, can_edit_only, chat_only. Returns array of teams with id, name, department, members count, and config flags. Use for assignment dropdowns, team-roster reports, and per-team triage scoping.
descriptions.team_get: Fetch one team by id. Required: id. Optional: includedetails (agent roster). Returns the team's name, department, agent list, default assignee, and out-of-hours config. Use to resolve team names or pull the team roster.
descriptions.team_list-tree: List teams as a hierarchical tree under their departments. Returns a nested array of departments → teams suitable for tree-view UIs and org-chart reports. Use when the flat `team_list` shape isn't enough.
descriptions.team_create: Create a HaloPSA team. Body is an array of team objects; minimum required field is name. Optional: department_id, agent_ids (members), default_assigneeid, custom_fields. Returns the new teams with ids.
descriptions.team_delete: Delete a team by id. Required: id. Destructive  -  agents lose this team membership; tickets currently assigned to this team need reassignment. Audit team membership before deleting.
descriptions.status_list: List HaloPSA ticket statuses (TStatus). Optional: domain, excludeclosed, excludepending. Returns array of statuses with id, name, type (open/pending/closed/cancelled), color, and order. Use as the lookup for status_id values referenced by `tickets_*` filters and updates. Status IDs are tenant-specific.
descriptions.status_get: Fetch one status by id. Required: id. Returns the full status definition including name, type, color, order, and any automation rules. Use to resolve status names from ticket payloads.
descriptions.priority_list: List HaloPSA priorities (Policy). Optional: includedistinct, slaid. Returns array of priorities with id, name, sla_response_time, sla_resolve_time, color, and is_default. Use as the lookup for priority_id values used in `tickets_*` filters/updates. Most tenants use 4-5 priority levels (Critical/High/Normal/Low/Planned).
descriptions.priority_get: Fetch one priority by id. Required: id. Returns the priority including SLA targets and metadata. Use to resolve priority names from ticket payloads.
